#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>

struct NetworkPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string username;
    std::vector<uint32_t> scores;
    std::string metadata;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream << packet_id;
        stream << username;
        stream << scores;
        stream.write(metadata, hexi::null_terminated);
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_packet(const NetworkPacket& packet) {
    hexi::BinaryStream stream;
    packet.serialise(stream);
    return std::vector<uint8_t>(stream.data(), stream.data() + stream.size());
}
```</answer>

#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct MessageHeader {
    uint16_t message_type;
    uint32_t body_length;
    uint32_t footer;
};

MessageHeader read_header_and_skip_body(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

std::vector<uint32_t> read_item_batch(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::stream_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::stream_type stream(adaptor);
    
    uint32_t item_count;
    stream >> item_count;
    
    std::vector<uint32_t> items;
    hexi::read_container(stream, items);
    
    return items;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

std::vector<uint8_t> read_buffer_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& source_buffer, size_t bytes_to_read) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<const uint8_t> adaptor(source_buffer.data(), source_buffer.size());
    std::vector<uint8_t> dest(bytes_to_read);
    adaptor.read(dest.data(), bytes_to_read);
    return dest;
}

void write_buffer_data(std::vector<uint8_t>& dest_buffer, const std::vector<uint8_t>& source_data) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<uint8_t> adaptor(dest_buffer.data(), dest_buffer.size());
    adaptor.write(source_data.data(), source_data.size());
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

std::vector<uint8_t> peek_buffer_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, size_t bytes_to_peek) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<const uint8_t> adaptor(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(bytes_to_peek);
    adaptor.copy(result.data(), bytes_to_peek);
    return result;
}

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>

#include <cstddef>

const uint8_t* get_read_pointer(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {

    return buffer.data();
}

struct BufferInfo {
    size_t readable_size;
    size_t free_space;

    BufferInfo info;
    info.readable_size = buffer.size();
    info.free_space = buffer.capacity() - buffer.size();
    return info;
}

#include <hexi/hexi.h>

#include <span>

struct MessagePacket {
    uint32_t header;
    std::string_view message;

MessagePacket read_header_and_message(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    uint32_t header = 0;
    adaptor.read(&header, sizeof(header));
    
    const uint8_t* message_start = adaptor.data() + adaptor.tell();
    const uint8_t* current = message_start;
    const uint8_t* end = buffer.data() + buffer.size();
    
    while (current < end && *current != 0) {
        ++current;
    }
    
    std::string_view message;
    if (current < end) {
        message = std::string_view(
            reinterpret_cast<const char*>(message_start),
            current - message_start
        );
    } else {
        message = "";
    }
    
    return {header, message};
}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/block_allocator.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <iostream>

template<size_t element_count = 10>
void demonstrate_block_allocator_behavior() {
    hexi::block_allocator<std::uint64_t, element_count> allocator;
    
    std::cout << "=== Block Allocator Free List Demonstration ===\n";
    std::cout << "Initial free blocks: " << element_count << "\n";
    
    // Allocate all blocks to empty the free list
    std::uint64_t* blocks[element_count];
    for (size_t i = 0; i < element_count; ++i) {
        blocks[i] = allocator.allocate();
        std::cout << "Allocated block " << i << " | Free blocks: " 
                  << (element_count - i - 1) << "\n";
    }
    
    // Attempt to allocate beyond capacity
    std::uint64_t* extra = allocator.allocate();
    if (!extra) {
        std::cout << "Correctly returned nullptr when free list is empty\n";
    }
    
    // Deallocate half the blocks to refill the free list
    size_t half = element_count / 2;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < half; ++i) {
        allocator.deallocate(blocks[i]);
        std::cout << "Deallocated block " << i << " | Free blocks: " 
                  << (half - i) << "\n";
    }
    
    // Allocate again after deallocation
    std::uint64_t* new_block = allocator.allocate();
    if (new_block) {
        std::cout << "Successfully allocated after deallocation\n";
    }
    
    // Clean up remaining blocks
    for (size_t i = half; i < element_count; ++i) {
        allocator.deallocate(blocks[i]);
    }
    allocator.deallocate(new_block);
    
    std::cout << "=== Demonstration Complete ===\n";
    std::cout << "Final free blocks: " << element_count << "\n";
}
```

**Explanation:**

This solution demonstrates the free list management of `block_allocator` by:

1. **Initialization**: Shows the initial state with `element_count` free blocks
2. **Allocation Phase**: Exhausts the free list by allocating all blocks
3. **Boundary Check**: Demonstrates proper handling of allocation requests when no blocks are available
4. **Deallocation Phase**: Refills the free list by deallocating half the blocks
5. **Re-allocation**: Shows successful allocation after deallocation
6. **Cleanup**: Properly deallocates all remaining blocks

The output illustrates how `push()` (via `deallocate()`) adds blocks to the free list and `pop()` (via `allocate()`) removes blocks from the free list, maintaining the internal free list structure as described in the problem.</answer>

#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <cstdint>

template<typename T>
T convert_to_big_endian(T value) {
    return hexi::endian::native_to_big(value);
}

template<typename T>
T convert_from_big_endian(T value) {
    return hexi::endian::big_to_native(value);
}

template<typename T>
T convert_to_little_endian(T value) {
    return hexi::endian::native_to_little(value);
}

template<typename T>
T convert_from_little_endian(T value) {
    return hexi::endian::little_to_native(value);
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <bit>

template<std::endian from, std::endian to, typename T>
T convert_endianness(T value) {

        return hexi::endian::swap_bytes(value);
    } else {
        return value;
    }
}

template<typename T>
T convert_endianness_runtime(T value, std::endian from, std::endian to) {
    if (from != to) {
        return hexi::endian::swap_bytes(value);
    }
    return value;
}
```

1. **`convert_endianness`**: This template function uses `if constexpr` to check if the source (`from`) and target (`to`) endianness differ at compile time. If they do, it swaps the bytes using `hexi::endian::swap_bytes`; otherwise, it returns the value unchanged.

2. **`convert_endianness_runtime`**: This function performs the same check but at runtime since the endianness parameters are passed dynamically. It uses `hexi::endian::swap_bytes` to swap bytes when necessary.

The solution assumes the existence of `hexi::endian::swap_bytes` for byte swapping. If the library uses a different API (e.g., `hexi::endian::convert`), adjust the function calls accordingly.</answer>

#include <hexi/file_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <filesystem>

std::vector<uint8_t> read_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, size_t bytes_to_read) {
    hexi::file_buffer file(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::read);
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer(bytes_to_read);
    size_t bytes_read = file.read(buffer.data(), bytes_to_read);
    buffer.resize(bytes_read);
    return buffer;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> preview_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, size_t bytes_to_preview) {
    hexi::file_buffer file(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::read);
    auto current_pos = file.tell();
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer(bytes_to_preview);
    size_t bytes_read = file.read(buffer.data(), bytes_to_preview);
    buffer.resize(bytes_read);
    file.seek(current_pos); // Restore original position
    return buffer;
}

void write_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) {
    hexi::file_buffer file(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::write);
    file.write(data.data(), data.size());
}

size_t get_file_size(const std::filesystem::path& file_path) {
    hexi::file_buffer file(file_path, hexi::file_buffer::read);
    auto size = file.seek(0, hexi::file_buffer::end); // Seek to end to get size
    file.seek(0, hexi::file_buffer::beg); // Seek back to beginning
    return size;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/file_buffer.h>
#include <filesystem>

size_t get_file_readable_size(const std::filesystem::path& file_path) {
    Hexi::file_buffer buf(file_path);
    if (!buf.is_valid()) {
        return 0;
    }
    return buf.size();
}

bool is_file_buffer_valid(const std::filesystem::path& file_path) {
    Hexi::file_buffer buf(file_path);
    return buf.is_valid();
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/static_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <algorithm>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
void write_data_to_buffer(hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, buffer_size>& buffer, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) {
    size_t current_size = buffer.size();
    size_t available_space = buffer_size - current_size;
    size_t bytes_to_write = std::min(data.size(), available_space);
    
    for (size_t i = 0; i < bytes_to_write; ++i) {
        buffer[i + current_size] = static_cast<std::byte>(data[i]);
    }
}

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
std::vector<uint8_t> read_data_from_buffer(hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, buffer_size>& buffer, size_t bytes_to_read) {
    size_t current_size = buffer.size();
    size_t bytes_available = std::min(bytes_to_read, current_size);
    
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(bytes_available);
    for (size_t i = 0; i < bytes_available; ++i) {
        result[i] = static_cast<uint8_t>(buffer[i]);
    }
    
    return result;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/static_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <algorithm>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
std::vector<uint8_t> preview_buffer_data(const hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, buffer_size>& buffer, size_t bytes_to_preview) {
    size_t read_pos = buffer.read_pos();
    size_t available = buffer.size() - read_pos;
    size_t preview_bytes = std::min(bytes_to_preview, available);
    
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(preview_bytes);
    const std::byte* data = buffer.read_ptr();
    std::copy(data, data + preview_bytes, result.begin());
    
    return result;

#include <hexi/static_buffer.h>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct UserData {
    uint32_t user_id;
    std::string name;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream << user_id << name;
    }
};

void serialize_object(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const UserData& data) {
    data.serialise(writer);
}

void deserialize_object(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader, UserData& data) {
    data.serialise(reader);
}
```

**Explanation:**
- The `serialize_object` function uses the `UserData`'s `serialise` method with a `binary_stream_writer` to write the object's data.
- The `deserialize_object` function similarly uses the `UserData`'s `serialise` method (which adapts to reading when given a `binary_stream_reader`) to reconstruct the object from serialized data.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <vector>

void write_string_to_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const std::string& str) {
    uint32_t length = static_cast<uint32_t>(str.size());
    writer.write(&length, sizeof(length));
    writer.write(str.data(), length);
}

std::string read_string_from_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader) {
    uint32_t length;
    reader.read(&length, sizeof(length));
    
    std::vector<char> buffer(length);
    reader.read(buffer.data(), length);
    
    return std::string(buffer.begin(), buffer.end());
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

void write_container_to_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const std::vector<uint32_t>& container) {
    // Write the count as a 32-bit unsigned integer
    uint32_t count = static_cast<uint32_t>(container.size());
    writer.write(count);
    
    // Write each element in the container
    for (const auto& element : container) {
        writer.write(element);
    }
}

std::vector<uint32_t> read_container_from_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader) {
    // Read the count first
    uint32_t count;
    reader.read(count);
    
    // Create a vector to hold the elements
    std::vector<uint32_t> result;
    result.reserve(count); // Reserve space for efficiency
    
    // Read each element and add to the vector
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < count; ++i) {
        uint32_t value;
        reader.read(value);
        result.push_back(value);
    }
    
    return result;
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

void write_data_to_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, uint32_t value, const void* data, std::size_t length) {
    // Write the integer value
    buffer.write(reinterpret_cast<const std::byte*>(&value), sizeof(value));
    // Write the byte array
    buffer.write(static_cast<const std::byte*>(data), length);
}

void read_data_from_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, uint32_t& value, void* data, std::size_t length) {
    // Read the integer value
    buffer.read(reinterpret_cast<std::byte*>(&value), sizeof(value));
    // Read the byte array
    buffer.read(static_cast<std::byte*>(data), length);
}

struct BufferInfo {
    std::size_t size;
    std::size_t free_space;
};

BufferInfo get_buffer_info(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    return {buffer.size(), buffer.free()};
}
```</answer>

#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

void skip_bytes_in_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::size_t bytes) {
    buffer.skip(bytes);
}

struct MessageHeader {

    uint32_t body_length;
};

MessageHeader read_header_and_skip_body(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {

    return header;

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

using namespace hexi::detail;

template<typename T>
std::size_t encode_varint(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, T value) {
    uint8_t byte;
    std::size_t bytes_written = 0;
    uint64_t unsigned_val = static_cast<uint64_t>(value);
    
    while (unsigned_val > 0x7F) {
        byte = (unsigned_val & 0x7F) | 0x80;
        writer.write(&byte, 1);
        bytes_written++;
        unsigned_val >>= 7;
    }
    
    byte = unsigned_val & 0x7F;
    writer.write(&byte, 1);
    bytes_written++;
    
    return bytes_written;
}

template<typename T>
T decode_varint(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader) {
    uint64_t result = 0;
    int shift = 0;
    uint8_t byte;
    
    do {
        reader.read(&byte, 1);
        result |= (byte & 0x7F) << shift;
        shift += 7;
    } while (byte & 0x80);
    
    return static_cast<T>(result);
}

template<typename T>
bool encode_decode_roundtrip(T original_value) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer writer(adaptor);
    hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader reader(adaptor);
    
    // Encode the value
    std::size_t bytes_written = encode_varint(writer, original_value);
    
    // Reset reader to the beginning of the buffer
    adaptor.seek(0);
    
    // Decode the value
    T decoded_value = decode_varint<T>(reader);
    
    return original_value == decoded_value;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <string_view>

void write_string_prefixed_varint(hexi::binary_stream& stream, const std::string& str) {
    stream << hexi::prefixed_varint(str);
}

std::string read_string_prefixed_varint(hexi::binary_stream& stream) {
    std::string s;
    stream >> hexi::prefixed_varint(s);
    return s;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <cstring>
#include <cstddef>

using namespace hexi::detail;

bool check_regions_overlap(const void* src, std::size_t src_len, const void* dst, std::size_t dst_len) {
    auto src_start = reinterpret_cast<uintptr_t>(src);
    auto src_end = src_start + src_len;
    auto dst_start = reinterpret_cast<uintptr_t>(dst);
    auto dst_end = dst_start + dst_len;
    return (src_start < dst_end) && (dst_start < src_end);
}

bool safe_memory_copy(void* destination, const void* source, std::size_t length) {
    if (length == 0) return true;
    
    // Check for overlap
    bool overlap = check_regions_overlap(source, length, destination, length);
    
    if (!overlap) {
        std::memcpy(destination, source, length);
        return true;
    }
    
    // Determine copy direction based on overlap
    const char* src_ptr = static_cast<const char*>(source);
    char* dest_ptr = static_cast<char*>(destination);
    
    auto src_start = reinterpret_cast<uintptr_t>(source);
    auto src_end = src_start + length;
    auto dest_start = reinterpret_cast<uintptr_t>(destination);
    
    if (dest_start >= src_start && dest_start < src_end) {
        // Copy backward to avoid overwriting source data
        for (std::size_t i = length; i > 0; --i) {
            dest_ptr[i-1] = src_ptr[i-1];
        }
    } else {
        // Copy forward
        std::memcpy(destination, source, length);
    }
    
    return true;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct CompactPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::vector<std::string> tags;
    std::string data;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_compact_packet(const CompactPacket& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    
    // Serialize packet_id as varint
    hexi::write_varint(buffer, packet.packet_id);
    
    // Serialize tags count using prefixed_varint
    hexi::write_prefixed_varint(buffer, packet.tags.size());
    
    // Serialize each tag
    for (const auto& tag : packet.tags) {
        hexi::write_prefixed_varint(buffer, tag.size());
        buffer.insert(buffer.end(), tag.begin(), tag.end());
    }
    
    // Serialize data size using prefixed_varint
    hexi::write_prefixed_varint(buffer, packet.data.size());
    
    // Append data
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), packet.data.begin(), packet.data.end());
    
    return buffer;
}

CompactPacket deserialize_compact_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    CompactPacket packet;
    size_t pos = 0;
    
    // Deserialize packet_id
    auto [id, bytes_read] = hexi::read_varint(buffer, pos);
    packet.packet_id = id;
    pos += bytes_read;
    
    // Deserialize tags count
    auto [tag_count, bytes_read] = hexi::read_prefixed_varint(buffer, pos);
    pos += bytes_read;
    packet.tags.reserve(tag_count);
    
    // Deserialize each tag
    for (size_t i = 0; i < tag_count; ++i) {
        auto [tag_size, bytes_read] = hexi::read_prefixed_varint(buffer, pos);
        pos += bytes_read;
        
        std::string tag(buffer.data() + pos, tag_size);
        packet.tags.push_back(std::move(tag));
        pos += tag_size;
    }
    
    // Deserialize data size
    auto [data_size, bytes_read] = hexi::read_prefixed_varint(buffer, pos);
    pos += bytes_read;
    
    std::string data(buffer.data() + pos, data_size);
    packet.data = std::move(data);
    
    return packet;
}

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <vector>

struct PaddedData {
    uint16_t header;
    std::string data;
    uint32_t footer;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_with_padding(const PaddedData& packet) {
    hexi::vector_buffer buffer;
    hexi::stream writer(buffer);
    
    writer.write(packet.header);
    writer.write(static_cast<uint16_t>(packet.data.size()));
    writer.write(packet.data);
    writer.write(hexi::fill<16>(0x00));
    writer.write(packet.footer);
    
    return buffer.to_vector();

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/block_allocator.h>
#include <vector>
#include <memory>

struct PoolObject {
    int id;
    double value;
    
    // Default constructor required by block_allocator
    PoolObject() : id(0), value(0.0) {}
    
    PoolObject(int i, double v) : id(i), value(v) {}
};

std::vector<PoolObject*> create_object_pool(hexi::block_allocator<PoolObject, 10>& allocator) {
    std::vector<PoolObject*> objects;
    
    // Allocate and construct three objects with different values
    for (int i = 1; i <= 3; ++i) {
        PoolObject* obj = static_cast<PoolObject*>(allocator.allocate(1));
        new (obj) PoolObject(i, i * 1.5);
        objects.push_back(obj);
    }
    
    return objects;
}
```</answer>

So the complete code would be:

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_base.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstddef>

class CustomBuffer : public hexi::pmc::buffer_base {
    std::vector<std::byte> data_;
    
public:
    std::size_t size() const override {
        return data_.size();
    }
    
    bool empty() const override {
        return data_.empty();
    }
    
    // Helper method to add data (for testing)
    void add_data(const void* source, std::size_t length) {
        const std::byte* bytes = static_cast<const std::byte*>(source);
        data_.insert(data_.end(), bytes, bytes + length);
    }
    
    // Helper method to clear data (for testing)
    void clear() {
        data_.clear();
    }
};
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_write_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct TypedData {
    uint32_t id;
    int16_t value;
    double ratio;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> write_typed_data(const TypedData& data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_write_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    adaptor.write(data.id);
    adaptor.write(data.value);
    adaptor.write(data.ratio);
    return buffer;
}

TypedData read_typed_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    TypedData data;
    adaptor.read(&data.id);
    adaptor.read(&data.value);
    adaptor.read(&data.ratio);
    return data;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_write_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

std::size_t write_multiple_chunks(const std::vector<std::vector<uint8_t>>& chunks) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_write_adaptor buf;
    std::size_t total_size = 0;
    for (const auto& chunk : chunks) {
        if (buf.free() >= chunk.size()) {
            buf.write(chunk.data(), chunk.size());
            total_size += chunk.size;
        }
    }
    return total_size;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> read_partial_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, std::size_t chunk_size) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor read_buf(buffer);
    std::vector<uint8_t> result;
    while (read_buf.size() > 0) {
        std::vector<uint8_t> chunk(chunk_size);
        std::size_t bytes_read = read_buf.read(chunk.data(), chunk_size);
        chunk.resize(bytes_read);
        result.insert(result.end(), chunk.begin(), chunk.end());
    }
    return result;
}
```</answer>

    return stream.get_buffer();
}

ProtocolPacket deserialize_protocol_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::binary_stream stream(buffer);
    
    // Read message_id and convert from big-endian to native
    uint32_t msg_id;
    stream.read(&msg_id, sizeof(msg_id));
    msg_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(msg_id);
    
    // Read data_values count and convert
    uint32_t count;
    stream.read(&count, sizeof(count));
    count = hexi::endian::big_to_native(count);
    
    // Read data_values using read_container, converting each element
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values;
    stream.read_container(data_values, 
                         [](uint16_t& value) { return hexi::endian::big_to_native(value); });
    
    // Read checksum and convert
    uint32_t checksum;
    stream.read(&checksum, sizeof(checksum));
    checksum = hexi::endian::big_to_native(checksum);
    
    return ProtocolPacket{msg_id, data_values, checksum};
}

ProtocolHeader extract_protocol_header(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    auto header_span = buffer.first(4);
    uint32_t msg_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(*reinterpret_cast<const uint32_t*>(header_span.data()));
    return ProtocolHeader{msg_id};
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <string>
#include <span>

struct MeasurementData {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::vector<float> temperatures;
    double pressure;
    
    void serialise(auto& adaptor) const {
        // Convert sensor_id to little-endian and write
        auto le_sensor_id = hexi::conditional_reverse(sensor_id, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
        adaptor.write(&le_sensor_id, sizeof(le_sensor_id));
        
        // Write temperatures count and each temperature (converted to little-endian)
        uint32_t temps_count = static_cast<uint32_t>(temperatures.size());
        adaptor.write(&temps_count, sizeof(temps_count));
        for (float temp : temperatures) {
            auto le_temp = hexi::conditional_reverse(temp, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
            adaptor.write(&le_temp, sizeof(le_temp));
        }
        
        // Convert pressure to little-endian and write
        auto le_pressure = hexi::conditional_reverse(pressure, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
        adaptor.write(&le_pressure, sizeof(le_pressure));
    }
    
    void deserialise(auto& adaptor) {
        // Read and convert sensor_id from little-endian
        uint32_t le_sensor_id;
        adaptor.read(&le_sensor_id, sizeof(le_sensor_id));
        hexi::little_to_native_inplace(le_sensor_id);
        sensor_id = le_sensor_id;
        
        // Read temperatures count and temperatures (converted from little-endian)
        uint32_t temps_count;
        adaptor.read(&temps_count, sizeof(temps_count));
        temperatures.resize(temps_count);
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < temps_count; ++i) {
            float le_temp;
            adaptor.read(&le_temp, sizeof(le_temp));
            hexi::little_to_native_inplace(le_temp);
            temperatures[i] = le_temp;
        }
        
        // Read and convert pressure from little-endian
        double le_pressure;
        adaptor.read(&le_pressure, sizeof(le_pressure));
        hexi::little_to_native_inplace(le_pressure);
        pressure = le_pressure;
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_measurement_data(const MeasurementData& data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::stream_write_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    data.serialise(adaptor);
    return buffer;
}

MeasurementData deserialize_measurement_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::stream_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    MeasurementData data;
    data.deserialise(adaptor);
    return data;
}

struct SensorInfo {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::string_view sensor_name;
};

SensorInfo extract_sensor_info(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::stream_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Extract null-terminated sensor name using view
    auto name_view = adaptor.view();
    std::string_view sensor_name(name_view.data(), name_view.size());
    
    // Enforce bounds and read sensor_id
    adaptor.enforce_read_bounds(sizeof(uint32_t));
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    adaptor.read(&sensor_id, sizeof(sensor_id));
    
    // Skip remaining data
    adaptor.skip(adaptor.remaining() - sizeof(sensor_id));
    
    return {sensor_id, sensor_name};
}
